_comment: Curated Schema.org type+property rules for IEO-launch-audit check 02 v0.5 (expanded v0.9, long-tail Article subtypes added v1.1, retired rich-results documented v1.2.1). NOT exhaustive vs schema.org/version/latest; covers the types this skill audits: Article (+ 15 subtypes: NewsArticle, BlogPosting, ScholarlyArticle, TechArticle, Report, AdvertiserContentArticle, OpinionNewsArticle, SatiricalArticle, BackgroundNewsArticle, AnalysisNewsArticle, AskPublicNewsArticle, ReportageNewsArticle, ReviewNewsArticle, SocialMediaPosting, DiscussionForumPosting — all inherit Article's required-property set), Person, Organization, WebSite, WebPage, BreadcrumbList, CollectionPage, ProfilePage, ImageObject, ItemList, ListItem, DefinedTerm, SpeakableSpecification, Occupation. Source: schema.org core vocabulary release notes through 2026-05. Update version field on each refresh so out-of-date drift is detectable.

_rich_result_retired_2026_01.purpose: Google retired rich-result display for 7 schema types in January 2026. The types remain VALID schema.org vocabulary (still parsed for entity understanding + AI engine extraction) — only the SERP rich-result UI is gone. If the consumer is emitting these types expecting rich-result eligibility, the audit should advise the type is parsed but not visually rendered.
_rich_result_retired_2026_01.audit_treatment: Detection is advisory only; the types are not deprecated. Cite this block when surfacing an INFO finding.

_faqpage_howto_2026_05.purpose: Google retired FAQ rich results May 2026 + removed FAQ from Rich Results Test June 2026 (HowTo deprecated earlier, 2023). The schema types remain LOAD-BEARING for IEO/GEO — ChatGPT, Perplexity, AI Overviews still parse FAQPage for Q&A extraction. The audit should SPLIT advice: 'deprecated for SERP-display visibility' (Google's stance) from 'still load-bearing for AI engine citation' (IEO best practice).
_faqpage_howto_2026_05.audit_treatment: If consumer emits FAQPage, do NOT flag as deprecated; advise the dual-stance framing.

_deprecated_types.GraphicNovel: Deprecated in schema.org v29.4 (Dec 2025); replaced by SequentialArt. Treat as a WARN with an offer-to-rename fix.

_web_validator.purpose: Long-tail @types (Recipe, Product, VideoObject, Event, FAQPage, HowTo, Course, JobPosting, LocalBusiness, etc.) are NOT covered by the offline curated rules above. v0.6 ships an opt-in web-validator fallback (check 2.10) that POSTs uncovered-type nodes to validator.schema.org for advisory validation.
_web_validator.endpoint: https://validator.schema.org/validate (unofficial; hosted by Google; no API contract; rate-limited; output shape may change without notice).
_web_validator.out_of_scope_offline: Recipe, Product, Offer, AggregateOffer, Review, AggregateRating, VideoObject, AudioObject, Event, FAQPage, Question, Answer, HowTo, HowToStep, Course, JobPosting, LocalBusiness, Service, Book, Movie, MusicRecording, TVSeries, SoftwareApplication, Dataset, MedicalEntity, ClaimReview — anything outside the editorial-site type set this skill audits. The fallback catches them at audit time without inflating the offline catalog.
_web_validator.should_be_covered_offline: BreadcrumbList ListItem item-shape (currently only the top-level rules are checked — ListItem now requires position + name + item, but the inner item-target shape is not validated). Organization + WebPage were promoted to offline rules in v0.9 (2026-05) once consumer-site usage justified the maintenance cost. Long-tail Article subtypes (AdvertiserContentArticle, OpinionNewsArticle, SatiricalArticle, BackgroundNewsArticle, AnalysisNewsArticle, AskPublicNewsArticle, ReportageNewsArticle, ReviewNewsArticle, SocialMediaPosting, DiscussionForumPosting) were promoted to offline rules in v1.1 (2026-05) — all inherit Article's required-prop set, so the maintenance cost is one shared rule template.
_web_validator.expansion_policy: Promote a type from web_validator fallback to offline curated rules when (a) a consumer site emits it on >5% of pages, and (b) the validator's findings have stabilised across 2+ audit runs without false-positives. Otherwise the fallback is preferable: it avoids committing this skill to maintain an exhaustive Schema.org catalog.

deprecated_properties.isReadableBy: Removed from core vocab; no replacement.
deprecated_properties.additionalType: Still valid but discouraged on Article; prefer multi-valued @type instead.
deprecated_properties.reviewBody: Use Review.reviewBody from Review type only; misuse on Article is invalid.
deprecated_properties.datePublishedOn: Never canonical; correct name is datePublished.
deprecated_properties.publishedOn: Never canonical; correct name is datePublished.
deprecated_properties.publishDate: Never canonical; correct name is datePublished.

value_type_notes.URL: string starting with http:// or https://, OR an object with @id that is a URL.
value_type_notes.URL-or-array-of-URLs: string URL or array of string URLs.
value_type_notes.URL-or-object-or-array: URL string, or {@id|@type|url} object, or array thereof.
value_type_notes.URL-or-object: URL string OR an object reference.
value_type_notes.integer: JSON number with no fractional component.
value_type_notes.string-or-array: JSON string or array of strings.
value_type_notes.string-or-array-or-object: JSON string, array of strings, or object/array of objects.
value_type_notes.object-or-array: JSON object OR array of objects.
value_type_notes.string-or-array: JSON string or array of strings.
